engineIntelligence.h2: Mazda Verisa Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the engines fitted to the Mazda Verisa in the UK - including known failure causes, production years across variants, typical replacement costs, and key technical specs. All prices reflect typical UK market ranges for rebuilt units.

cards.altText: Mazda Verisa ZY-VE engine - petrol 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - documented at 80,000-100,000 km on ZY engines.
Valve cover gasket leak - common oil seepage point on ageing imports.
Oxygen sensor failure - frequent MOT-related issue on high-mileage units.

cards.altText: Mazda Verisa ZY-VE CVT engine - petrol 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: CVT transmission judder - belt wear causes hesitation on high-mileage imports.
Timing chain tensioner wear - linked to rattle and stretch at 80k-100k km.
Oil leaks from timing cover - common on 10+ year old imported units.

cards.altText: Mazda Verisa ZY-VE 4WD engine - petrol 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Different oil pan fitment - 4WD variants require specific replacement components.
Timing chain stretch - same ZY engine issue at 80,000-100,000 km.
Drivetrain component wear - AWD system adds complexity to replacement.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,800-£2,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable ZY-VE units - actual quotes may vary for 4WD fitment.

commonProblems.h2: Mazda Verisa Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mazda Verisa's ZY engine family is generally robust, but UK owners commonly report timing chain stretch, CVT transmission issues, and oil leaks on high-mileage JDM imports. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Why Mazda Verisa ZY Engines Fail at High Mileage
problems.affectedModels: Mazda Verisa 15C, 15F, 15S, 15T (2004-2015, ZY-VE petrol); 15SD (2004-2007, ZY-DE diesel)
problems.rootCause: Chain tensioner wear and guide degradation on the ZY engine family, accelerated by infrequent oil changes or poor-quality lubricants common in JDM import history.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ZY-VE with upgraded chain components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Verisas valued £2k-5k; addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: On a Mazda Verisa worth £2,000-5,000, a full rebuilt engine at £1,800-2,200 often represents better long-term value than preventative chain work, especially if the vehicle has unknown JDM service history. We recommend replacement if chain rattle is audible.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ZY-VE engine prices for your Mazda Verisa

problems.h4: CVT Transmission Failure - Common on High-Mileage Mazda Verisa Imports
problems.affectedModels: Mazda Verisa CVT automatic variants (2004-2015, ZY-VE petrol)
problems.rootCause: CVT belt wear and pulley degradation on high-mileage Japanese imports, exacerbated by stop-start urban driving and infrequent fluid changes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain/refill CVT fluid, reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may delay failure but not resolve wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt ZY + CVT Assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ZY-VE engine with refurbished CVT, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for CVT-failed Verisas

problems.recommendation: CVT failures on the Verisa are often terminal for the transmission alone. Given the vehicle's residual value, we typically recommend sourcing a complete rebuilt ZY+CVT assembly rather than attempting standalone CVT repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mazda Verisa CVT engine replacement

problems.h4: Oil Leaks & Gasket Failure - Ageing Mazda Verisa ZY Engines
problems.affectedModels: All Mazda Verisa variants (2004-2015, ZY family engines)
problems.rootCause: Rubber gasket degradation on valve cover and timing cover due to age, heat cycles, and potential use of non-specification oils during JDM ownership.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve cover/timing cover gaskets, clean oil residue
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for minor leaks; doesn't address underlying wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ZY-VE with all new gaskets, seals, upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Best for multiple leaks or high-mileage units; comprehensive solution

problems.recommendation: For isolated minor leaks on otherwise healthy Verisas, gasket replacement is cost-effective. However, if multiple leaks exist alongside high mileage, a rebuilt engine often provides better long-term reliability and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare Mazda Verisa ZY engine rebuild options

problems.h4: Oxygen Sensor Failure - MOT-Related Mazda Verisa Issue
problems.affectedModels: All Mazda Verisa variants (2004-2015, ZY family engines)
problems.rootCause: Oxygen sensor degradation from age, fuel quality variations in Japan, and exposure to road salts during UK use, leading to failed emissions tests.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty upstream/downstream O2 sensor, clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for isolated sensor failure; quick MOT fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ZY-VE with new sensors, comprehensive emissions system check
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if sensor failure accompanies other engine issues

problems.recommendation: Oxygen sensor failure is typically a straightforward, low-cost repair. However, if the sensor failure coincides with rough running, poor fuel economy, or other ZY engine symptoms, consider a full engine assessment before proceeding.
problems.cta: Get Mazda Verisa engine diagnostic quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mazda Verisa
finalCta.paragraph: The Mazda Verisa's niche JDM import status means specialist knowledge is critical for accurate diagnosis and cost-effective replacement. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand ZY engine quirks, CVT compatibility, and grey import documentation. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from vetted specialists and get back on the road without main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mazda Verisa Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mazda Verisa and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mazda Verisa Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mazda Verisa's 2004-2015 production timeline, organised so UK grey import owners can quickly identify their ZY engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Mazda Verisa launched in May 2004 with the ZY engine family, establishing the 1.5L petrol and rare diesel options that would define the model throughout its UK import life.
keyChanges: Mazda Verisa introduced to Japanese market (May 2004)
ZY-DE 1.5L diesel offered exclusively in 15SD trim (2004-2007 only)
DC5W (2WD) and rare DC5R (4WD) chassis variants established
knownFor: Launch-era JDM imports now reaching 15-20 years old in UK
Early ZY engines show timing chain wear at 80k-100k km
Unknown Japanese service history complicates maintenance planning
CVT automatic transmissions common on high-mileage imports
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2009 Mazda Verisa replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda Verisa ZY engine replacement • Mazda Verisa timing chain fix • Mazda Verisa CVT failure cost

years.description: The 2010 facelift refreshed the Verisa's exterior styling while retaining the proven ZY engine family, with production winding down through 2012 for most export-bound units.
keyChanges: Exterior facelift with revised front/rear styling (2010)
Interior trim updates and equipment revisions
ZY-VE turbo variant (15T) remained available for performance buyers
Final production year for diesel 15SD variant (2007 end, but units exported later)
knownFor: Facelift models command slight premium in UK import market
Cosmetic parts differ from pre-facelift; engines remain interchangeable
Lower mileage examples more likely from this production window
ZY engine reliability unchanged; same failure patterns apply
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Mazda Verisa replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda Verisa facelift engine • Mazda Verisa 15T turbo replacement • Mazda Verisa ZY compatibility

years.description: Mazda Verisa production concluded in 2015 with the ZY engine family unchanged, making late-model imports the most desirable for UK buyers seeking lower-mileage replacement candidates.
keyChanges: Final production run focused on domestic Japanese demand
No further engine updates; ZY-VE remained sole petrol option
Model discontinued without direct successor in Mazda's compact lineup
Last units exported to UK grey import market through 2015-2016
knownFor: Lowest mileage examples typically from this final production window
Same ZY engine architecture ensures parts compatibility across all years
Higher purchase price for late-model imports offsets potential repair costs
Ideal candidates for preventative timing chain service on arrival
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2015 Mazda Verisa replacement engines

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mazda Verisa was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact ZY engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mazda Verisa specialists.

faq.h2: Mazda Verisa Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mazda Verisa engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mazda Verisa ZY-VE 1.5L petrol engine typically costs £1,800-£2,200 supply-only in the UK, with rare ZY-DE diesel units ranging £2,400-£2,800. Actual quotes vary by supplier, engine condition and whether supply & fit is included.
keyPoints: Rebuilt ZY-VE petrol: £1,800-£2,200 supply only
Rare ZY-DE diesel: £2,400-£2,800 due to scarcity
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 depending on garage location
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Mazda Verisa engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mazda Verisa engine?
items.answer: For Mazda Verisas valued at £2,000-5,000, a £1,800-2,200 rebuilt ZY engine can be worthwhile if the chassis, transmission and body are in good condition. Consider total vehicle value versus replacement cost before proceeding.
keyPoints: Verisa residual values typically £2,000-5,000 in UK
Rebuilt ZY-VE cost £1,800-£2,200 plus fitting if required
Factor in transmission, suspension and body condition
Specialist JDM knowledge essential for accurate assessment
items.cta: Get a Mazda Verisa engine valuation quote

items.question: What causes timing chain problems on Mazda Verisa ZY engines?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch on Mazda Verisa ZY engines typically occurs at 80,000-100,000 km due to tensioner wear and guide degradation, often accelerated by infrequent oil changes or poor-quality lubricants during Japanese ownership.
keyPoints: Documented failure point at 80k-100k km on ZY family
Tensioner and guide wear primary mechanical causes
Audible rattle or rattling noise indicates imminent failure
Preventative replacement cheaper than post-failure engine damage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt ZY engine prices for your Mazda Verisa

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mazda Verisa?
items.answer: Yes - the Mazda Verisa's ZY engine is shared with the Mazda Demio/Mazda2, Mazda3, Premacy and Axela, expanding compatible replacement options. However, chassis code (DC5W vs DC5R) and transmission type must be verified for correct fitment.
keyPoints: ZY engine fits multiple Mazda models with same architecture
DC5W (2WD) and DC5R (4WD) require different oil pan components
CVT vs manual transmission affects compatibility checks
Specialist verification recommended before purchasing replacement
items.cta: Check Mazda Verisa engine compatibility now

items.question: What warranty comes with a Mazda Verisa replacement engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt or reconditioned Mazda Verisa engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, though exact terms should be confirmed with your chosen supplier before purchase.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Supply & fit packages often include installation warranty
Always obtain written warranty terms before proceeding
items.cta: Find Mazda Verisa engines with warranty coverage

items.question: How long does Mazda Verisa engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most UK specialists complete a Mazda Verisa engine replacement within 5-10 working days, accounting for sourcing the correct ZY engine, shipping from JDM suppliers, and fitting by technicians familiar with grey import chassis codes.
keyPoints: Typical turnaround: 5-10 working days end-to-end
Sourcing rare variants (4WD, diesel, turbo) may extend timeline
Supply-only orders ship within 48 hours of confirmation
Supply & fit bookings require garage availability coordination
items.cta: Get a Mazda Verisa engine replacement timeline quote

items.question: How do I identify my Mazda Verisa engine code?
items.answer: Your Mazda Verisa's engine code (ZY, ZY-VE, ZY-DE) can be identified via the vehicle's chassis plate, registration lookup via DVLA, or by consulting Japanese import documentation - critical for ordering the correct replacement unit.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near timing cover
DVLA registration check confirms variant and fuel type
Japanese export certificate lists original engine specification
Chassis code DC5W (2WD) vs DC5R (4WD) affects oil pan fitment
items.cta: Verify your Mazda Verisa engine code instantly

items.question: Are Mazda Verisa diesel engines reliable?
items.answer: The rare ZY-DE diesel engine in Mazda Verisa 15SD models (2004-2007) shares the ZY family's generally robust design but suffers from typical small-diesel issues like injector wear and EGR clogging, with limited UK parts availability due to scarcity.
keyPoints: ZY-DE diesel only fitted 2004-2007 to 15SD trim
Injector wear and EGR issues common on high-mileage units
Extremely limited UK availability complicates parts sourcing
Rebuilt cost £2,400-£2,800 reflects rarity premium
items.cta: Compare Mazda Verisa diesel engine options

items.question: Can CVT transmission issues be fixed without replacing the whole engine?
items.answer: CVT transmission problems on Mazda Verisa can sometimes be addressed with fluid service or standalone transmission repair, but high-mileage JDM imports often require complete ZY+CVT assembly replacement for reliable, warrantied results.
keyPoints: CVT fluid service may delay but not resolve belt wear
Standalone CVT repair possible but limited specialist availability
Complete rebuilt ZY+CVT assembly often best value for failed units
Verify transmission type before ordering replacement engine
items.cta: Get Mazda Verisa CVT engine replacement quotes

items.question: Do you deliver Mazda Verisa engines nationwide?
items.answer: Yes - EngineMarket's UK network of Mazda Verisa specialists offers nationwide delivery of rebuilt, reconditioned and used ZY engines, typically within 48 hours of order confirmation for supply-only purchases.

Supply-only orders ship within 48 hours typically
